content:
@Miguel Ojeda for the Fedora Asahi kernel builds, we adapted for newer bindgen, but things are choking on Rust 1.66. :frown:

Do you know when Rust 1.66 support will become available in the R4L tree?

At the moment I've got a working build by using this patch

diff --git a/rust/alloc/alloc.rs b/rust/alloc/alloc.rs
index ca224a541770..dac9e96d38c3 100644
--- a/rust/alloc/alloc.rs
+++ b/rust/alloc/alloc.rs
@@ -30,13 +30,13 @@ extern "Rust" {
     // The rustc fork of LLVM also special-cases these function names to be able to optimize them
     // like `malloc`, `realloc`, and `free`, respectively.
     #[rustc_allocator]
-    #[rustc_allocator_nounwind]
+    #[rustc_nounwind]
     fn __rust_alloc(size: usize, align: usize) -> *mut u8;
-    #[rustc_allocator_nounwind]
+    #[rustc_nounwind]
     fn __rust_dealloc(ptr: *mut u8, size: usize, align: usize);
-    #[rustc_allocator_nounwind]
+    #[rustc_nounwind]
     fn __rust_realloc(ptr: *mut u8, old_size: usize, align: usize, new_size: usize) -> *mut u8;
-    #[rustc_allocator_nounwind]
+    #[rustc_nounwind]
     fn __rust_alloc_zeroed(size: usize, align: usize) -> *mut u8;
 }

diff --git a/rust/alloc/boxed.rs b/rust/alloc/boxed.rs
index 8fd296421dec..81b6ab4d8137 100644
--- a/rust/alloc/boxed.rs
+++ b/rust/alloc/boxed.rs
@@ -1971,8 +1971,7 @@ impl<T: ?Sized, A: Allocator> AsMut<T> for Box<T, A> {
  *  could have a method to project a Pin<T> from it.
  */
 #[stable(feature = "pin", since = "1.33.0")]
-#[rustc_const_unstable(feature = "const_box", issue = "92521")]
-impl<T: ?Sized, A: Allocator> const Unpin for Box<T, A> where A: 'static {}
+impl<T: ?Sized, A: Allocator> Unpin for Box<T, A> where A: 'static {}

 #[unstable(feature = "generator_trait", issue = "43122")]
 impl<G: ?Sized + Generator<R> + Unpin, R, A: Allocator> Generator<R> for Box<G, A>
diff --git a/rust/kernel/driver.rs b/rust/kernel/driver.rs
index 7154c0221e40..af42a773256e 100644
--- a/rust/kernel/driver.rs
+++ b/rust/kernel/driver.rs
@@ -119,6 +119,7 @@ impl<T: DriverOps> Drop for Registration<T> {
 ///   - [`RawDeviceId::ZERO`] is actually a zeroed-out version of the raw device id.
 ///   - [`RawDeviceId::to_rawid`] stores `offset` in the context/data field of the raw device id so
 ///     that buses can recover the pointer to the data.
+#[const_trait]
 pub unsafe trait RawDeviceId {
     /// The raw type that holds the device id.
     ///

Though this is a kludge and not really address the issue that the alloc crate is up to date.

@Miguel Ojeda Well, it fixed my build, though I think this generates some work for @Asahi Lina to fix her Asahi GPU driver code to work with Rust 1.66, since it's causing Xorg to crash and take down GDM now with my kernel build incorporating this change. It's entirely possible that it's related to other jumps and changes with unstable Rust features she uses that mainline Linux Rust doesn't use.
What did you mean by "but things are choking on Rust 1.66", by the way?
Is it related to the Reported-by you are suggesting? i.e. we were planning to move to 1.66.0 since a while ago.
@Miguel Ojeda currently testing your Kernelci PR

@Miguel Ojeda Nah, the tests were with the wrong kernel build, everything is fine! :sweat_smile:

I've updated accordingly: https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/pull/947#pullrequestreview-1230765595
Also I needed a tweak to fix bindgen calls : https://gitlab.com/fedora-asahi/kernel-asahi/-/commit/7407fdf7716f3233da56a5fb4e2e28fffb775fa5
@Miguel Ojeda merged Rust 1.66.0 on KernelCI https://github.com/kernelci/kernelci-core/pull/1597
